name: ts-rest (Full-Stack TypeScript Contract System)

auth.notes: Framework library; auth handled via middleware

performance.notes: Near-zero overhead; thin wrapper over HTTP

agent_readiness.notes: tRPC-like contract system for REST APIs (not RPC). Define contract with initContract().router() using Zod schemas for requests/responses. Server implementations: Express, Fastify, Next.js, Nest.js. Client auto-typed from contract. Generates OpenAPI spec. Works across HTTP (unlike tRPC). Great for public APIs needing both TypeScript safety and REST compatibility. Gaining popularity as REST alternative to tRPC. @ts-rest/react-query for react-query integration.

gotchas: Larger setup than tRPC for internal TypeScript-to-TypeScript APIs
Complex nested routers can be verbose
best_when: Public REST APIs needing TypeScript safety client-to-server
When tRPC's RPC model doesn't fit (need REST semantics)
APIs that must be consumable outside TypeScript ecosystem
Teams wanting OpenAPI spec generation from TypeScript contracts
summary: ts-rest provides tRPC-style type safety for REST APIs — define contracts with Zod schemas, implement on Express/Fastify/Next.js, consume with auto-typed client. Unlike tRPC it uses REST semantics so APIs are HTTP-standard and can generate OpenAPI specs. Best for public or cross-language REST APIs wanting full TypeScript safety. tRPC is simpler for purely internal TypeScript-to-TypeScript communication.
